title: Introduction to pvEBayes
text: Contemporary drug postmarketing surveillance largely relies collection spontaneous reports suspected adverse drug events pharmaceutical companies, healthcare professionals, patients. reports curated stored spontaneous reporting systems (SRS), usually organized large frequency table downstream data analysis. consider SRS dataset cataloging adverse event (AE) reports II AE rows across JJ drug columns. Let NijN_{ij} denote number reported cases ii-th AE jj-th drug, =1,...,Ii = 1,..., j=1,...,Jj = 1,..., J. Therefore, AE-drug pairwise occurrences AE-reports summarized ×JI \times J contingency table, (,j)(,j)-th cell catalogs observed count NijN_{ij} indicating number cases involving ii-th AE jj-th drug. Current disproportionality analysis mainly focuses signal detection seeks determine whether observation NijN_{ij} substantially greater corresponding null baseline EijE_{ij}. equivalently, signal strength λij\lambda_{ij} significantly greater 1. addition signal detection, Tan et al. (Stat. Med., 2025) broaden role disproportionality signal strength estimation. use flexible non-parametric empirical Bayes models enables nuanced empirical Bayes posterior inference (signal strength {λij}\{\lambda_{ij}\} estimation uncertainty quantification). allows researchers distinguish AE-drug pairs appear similar binary signal detection framework. example, AE-drug pairs signal strengths 1.5 4.0 significantly greater 1 detected signal. differences signal strength may distinct implications medical clinical contexts. Methods implemented package assumes observed count NijN_{ij} conditional EijE_{ij} Nij∣Eij∼Poisson⁡(Eijλij),Nij=0,1,2…,\begin{equation} \label{eqn:model-poisson-likelihood} N_{ij} \mid E_{ij} \sim \operatorname{Poisson}(E_{ij}\lambda_{ij}), \ N_{ij} = 0, 1, 2 \dots,   \end{equation} parameter λij≥0\lambda_{ij} \geq 0 relative reporting ratio, signal strength, (,j)(, j)-th pair measuring ratio actual expected count arising due dependence null baseline expected count. Therefore, {λij}\{\lambda_{ij}\} key parameters interest. large λij\lambda_{ij} indicates strong association drug AE. Let gg prior density function signal strength parameters AE-drug pairs: λij∼g\lambda_{ij} \sim g. , context Poisson model, marginal probability mass function NijN_{ij} given : p(Nij)=∫0∞g(λij)fpois(Nij∣λijEij)dλij, p(N_{ij}) = \int_0^{\infty} g(\lambda_{ij}) \ f_{\text{pois}}(N_{ij} \mid \lambda_{ij}E_{ij}) \ d\lambda_{ij},  fpois(Nij∣λij)f_{\text{pois}}(N_{ij} \mid \lambda_{ij}) probability mass function Poisson random variable mean λij\lambda_{ij} evaluated NijN_{ij}. empirical Bayes framework, prior distribution consequently estimated data maximizing log marginal likelihood: ĝ=argmaxg∑=1I∑j=1Jlog⁡p(Nij). \hat g = \mathop{\arg \max}\limits_{g} \sum_{=1}^\sum_{j=1}^J \log p(N_{ij}).  , estimated empirical Bayes posterior density λij\lambda_{ij} given NijN_{ij} : p̂(λij∣Nij)=ĝ(λij)fpois(Nij∣λijEij)p̂(Nij),λij>0, \hat{\text{p}}(\lambda_{ij} \mid N_{ij}) = \frac{\hat g(\lambda_{ij}) f_{\text{pois}}(N_{ij} \mid \lambda_{ij} E_{ij})}{\hat{\text{p}}(N_{ij})}, \ \lambda_{ij} > 0,  p̂(Nij)=∫0∞ĝ(λij)fpois(Nij∣λijEij)dλij\hat{\text{p}}(N_{ij}) = \int_0^{\infty} \hat g(\lambda_{ij})f_{\text{pois}}(N_{ij}\mid \lambda_{ij}E_{ij}) \ d\lambda_{ij}. empirical Bayes models implemented ‘pvEBayes’ share structure described ; differ assumptions prior distribution. Implemented methods include Gamma-Poisson Shrinker (GPS), Koenker-Mizera (KM) method, Efron’s nonparametric empirical Bayes approach, K-gamma model, general-gamma model. selection prior distribution critical Bayesian analysis. GPS model uses gamma mixture prior assuming signal/non-signal structure SRS data. However, real-world setting, signal strengths (λij){(\lambda_{ij})} often heterogeneous thus follows multi-modal distribution, making difficult assume parametric prior. Non-parametric empirical Bayes models (KM, Efron, K-gamma general-gamma) address challenge utilizing flexible prior general mixture form estimating prior distribution data-driven way. KM method existing implementation REBayes package, relies Mosek, commercial convex optimization solver, may limit accessibility due licensing issues. pvEBayes package provides alternative fully open-source implementation KM method using CVXR. Efron’s method also general nonparametric empirical Bayes implementation deconvolveR package; however, implementation support exposure offset parameter Poisson model, corresponds expected null value Eij{E_{ij}}. pvEBayes, implementation Efron’s method adapted modified deconvolveR support Eij{E_{ij}} Poisson model. addition, package implements novel bi-level Expectation Conditional Maximization (ECM) algorithm proposed Tan et al. (2025) efficient parameter estimation gamma mixture prior based models mentioned . Package ‘pvEBayes’ provides suite streamlined tools effectively fitting models spontaneous reporting system (SRS) frequency tables, extracting summaries, performing hyperparameter tuning, generating graphical summaries (eye plots heatmaps) signal detection estimation. following provide example, borrowed Tan et al. (arxiv, 2025), SRS data analyzing ‘pvEBayes’.

See Tan et al. (2025, ) details. illustration, fit model fixed hyperparameter value α=0.5\alpha = 0.5 using pvEBayes() function. convergence criterion pvEBayes() based relative change log likelihood consecutive iterations. example, algorithm stopped satisfying convergence criterion. also reflected printed model fitting message, optimizer convergence status reported successful. can inspect number iterations used fitting algorithm gg_given_alpha\$iter. fit, convergence achieved 167 iterations. next example illustrates case algorithm stops reaches maximum number iterations. , use smaller convergence tolerance. case, algorithm stopped reaching specified maximum 200 iterations. title: Empirical Bayes Methods for Pharmacovigilance
text: pvEBayes R package implements suite nonparametric empirical Bayes methods pharmacovigilance, including Gamma-Poisson Shrinker (GPS), K-gamma, general-gamma, Koenker-Mizera (KM), Efron models. provides tools fitting models spontaneous reporting system (SRS) frequency tables, extracting summaries, performing hyperparameter tuning, generating graphical summaries (eye plots heatmaps) signal detection signal strength estimation. package perform SRS frequency table preprocessing, raw individual case safety reports (ICSRs) aggregrating. steps handled using pvEBayes. Spontaneous Reporting System (SRS) Table: drug safety SRS dataset catalogs AE reports AE rows across J drug columns. Let Nij{N_{ij}} denote number reported cases -th AE j-th drug, =1,...,{= 1,..., } j=1,...,J{j = 1,..., J}. Empirical Bayes modeling SRS data mining: Model AE-drug count Nij∼Poisson(λijEij)N_{ij} \sim \text{Poisson}(\lambda_{ij} E_{ij}), Nij=0,1,2,…N_{ij} = 0, 1, 2, \dots EijE_{ij}: expected baseline value assuming AE-drug association. λij>0\lambda_{ij} > 0: relative reporting ratio / signal strength (,j)-th AE-drug pair (multiplicative deviation null baseline value). signal detection signal strength λ\lambda estimation Traditional SRS data mining emphasizes signal detection: identify AE-drug pairs observed counts substantially larger null value, .e., λij>1\lambda_{ij} > 1. Tan et al. (Stat. Med., 2025) extend signal strength estimation: estimate {λij}\{\lambda_{ij}\} quantify uncertainty via flexible nonparametric empirical Bayes posterior distribution. Signal estimation helps distinguish AE-drug pairs look identical binary signal detection framework (e.g., λ=1.5\lambda=1.5 vs λ=4.0\lambda=4.0), can different clinical implications. Methods implemented pvEBayes (differ prior assumptions): Gamma-Poisson Shrinker (GPS) Koenker–Mizera method (KM) Efron’s nonparametric empirical Bayes approach K-gamma method General-gamma method nonparametric priors? GPS uses 2 gamma mixture prior motivated signal/non-signal structure. Real-world signal strengths λij\lambda_{ij} can heterogeneous, underlying (prior) distribution λij\lambda_{ij} may multi-modal multiple distinct peaks, making simple parametric priors hard justify. Nonparametric empirical Bayes methods (KM, Efron, general-gamma) address challenge utilizing flexible prior general mixture form estimating prior distribution data-driven way. Implementation highlights: Provides fully open-source KM implementation using CVXR (avoids reliance commercial Mosek solver used REBayes). Adapts Efron’s approach deconvolveR support exposure/offset EijE_{ij} Poisson model (supported original implementation). Implements bi-level Expectation Conditional Maximization (ECM) algorithm Tan et al. (Stat. Med., 2025) prior estimation gamma-mixture prior based models (GPS, K-gamma, general-gamma). Supports AIC/BIC-based hyperparameter tuning α\alpha general-gamma model (p,c0)(p, c_0) Efron’s approach (see Tan et al.  (Stat. Med., 2025) detail). detailed methodological description, see Tan et al.  (Stat. Med., 2025).

what: Generate an eyeplot showing the distribution of posterior draws for selected drugs and adverse events — eyeplot_pvEBayes
title: Generate an eyeplot showing the distribution of posterior draws for selected drugs and adverse events — eyeplot_pvEBayes
text: function creates eyeplot visualize posterior distributions \(\lambda_{ij}\) selected AEs drugs. plot displays posterior median, 90 percent credible interval selected AE-drug combination.

title: Generate an eyeplot showing the distribution of posterior draws for selected drugs and adverse events — eyeplot_pvEBayes
code: eyeplot_pvEBayes(   x,   num_top_AEs = 10,   num_top_drugs = 8,   specified_AEs = NULL,   specified_drugs = NULL,   N_threshold = 1,   text_shift = 4,   x_lim_scalar = 1.3,   text_size = 3,   log_scale = FALSE )

title: Generate an eyeplot showing the distribution of posterior draws for selected drugs and adverse events — eyeplot_pvEBayes
text: x pvEBayes object, output function pvEBayes pvEBayes_tune. num_top_AEs number significant AEs appearing plot. Default 10. num_top_drugs number significant drugs appearing plot. Default 7. specified_AEs vector AE names specified appear plot. vector AEs given, argument num_top_AEs ignored. specified_drugs vector drug names specified appear plot. vector drugs given, argument num_top_drugs ignored. N_threshold integer greater 0. AE-drug combination observation smaller N_threshold filtered . text_shift numeric. Controls relative position text labels, (e.g., "N = 1", "E = 2"). larger value shifts "E = 2" away original position. x_lim_scalar numeric. x-axis range scalar ensures text labels appropriately included plot. text_size numeric. Controls size text labels, (e.g., "N = 1", "E = 2"). log_scale logical. TRUE, eye plot displays posterior distribution \(\log(\lambda_{ij})\) selected AEs drugs.

title: A suite of empirical Bayes methods to use in pharmacovigilance. — pvEBayes-package
text: pvEBayes provides collection parametric non-parametric empirical Bayes methods implementation pharmacovigilance (including signal detection signal estimation) spontaneous reporting systems (SRS) data. SRS dataset catalogs AE reports AE rows across J drug columns. Let \(N_{ij}\) denote number reported cases -th AE j-th drug, \(= 1,..., \) \(j = 1,..., J\). assume AE-drug pair, \(N_{ij} \sim \text{Poisson}(\lambda_{ij} E_{ij})\), \(E_{ij}\) expected baseline value measuring expected count AE-drug pair association -th AE j-th drug. parameter \(\lambda_{ij} \geq 0\) represents relative reporting ratio, signal strength, \((, j)\)-th pair measuring ratio actual expected count arising due dependence null baseline expected count. Current disproportionality analysis mainly focuses signal detection seeks determine whether observation \(N_{ij}\) substantially greater corresponding null baseline \(E_{ij}\). Poisson model, say, signal strength \(\lambda_{ij}\) significantly greater 1. addition signal detection, Tan et al. (Stat. Med., 2025) broaden role disproportionality signal estimation. use flexible non-parametric empirical Bayes models enables nuanced empirical Bayes posterior inference (parameter estimation uncertainty quantification) signal strength parameter \(\{ \lambda_{ij} \}\). allows researchers distinguish AE-drug pairs appear similar binary signal detection framework. example, AE-drug pairs signal strengths 1.5 4.0 significantly greater 1 detected signal. differences signal strength may distinct implications medical clinical contexts. methods included pvEBayes differ assumptions prior distribution. Implemented methods include Gamma-Poisson Shrinker (GPS), Koenker-Mizera (KM) method, Efron’s nonparametric empirical Bayes approach, K-gamma model, general-gamma model. GPS model uses two gamma mixture prior assuming signal/non-signal structure SRS data. However, real-world setting, signal strengths \((\lambda_{ij})\) often heterogeneous thus follows multi-modal distribution, making difficult assume parametric prior. Non-parametric empirical Bayes models (KM, Efron, K-gamma general-gamma) address challenge utilizing flexible prior general mixture form estimating prior distribution data-driven way. pvEBayes offers first implemention bi-level Expectation Conditional Maximization (ECM) algorithm proposed Tan et al. (2025) efficient parameter estimation gamma mixture prior based models: GPS K-gamma general-gamma. KM method existing implementation REBayes package, relies Mosek, commercial convex optimization solver, may limit accessibility due licensing issue. pvEBayes provides alternative fully open-source implementation KM method using CVXR. Efron’s method also general nonparametric empirical Bayes implementation deconvolveR package; however, implementation support exposure offset parameter Poisson model, corresponds expected null value \(E_{ij}\). pvEBayes, implementation Efron's method adapted modified deconvolveR support \(E_{ij}\) Poisson model. detailed introduction pvEBayes, see Tan et al. code: set.seed(1)  # fit general-gamma model with a specified alpha fit <- pvEBayes(   contin_table = statin2025_44,   model = "general-gamma",   alpha = 0.3,   n_posterior_draws = 1000 ) #> ℹ Fitting general-gamma model... #> ✔ Fitting general-gamma model... [126ms] #>  #> ℹ Generating 1000 posterior draws... #> ✔ Generating 1000 posterior draws... [24ms] #>  #> Object of class 'pvEBayes' #>  #> General-gamma model with hyperparameter alpha = 0.3. #> Estimated prior is a mixture of 18 gamma distributions. #>  #> Running time of the general-gamma model fitting: 0.1312 seconds. #> Optimizer convergence: successful. #> Running time for posterior draws  #> (1000 signal strength posterior draws per AE-drug pair):0.0286 seconds. #>  #> Extract estimated prior parameters, discovered signals #> and signal strength posterior draws using `summary()`.  # fit K-gamma model with K = 3 fit_Kgamma <- pvEBayes(   contin_table = statin2025_44, model = "K-gamma",   K = 3, n_posterior_draws = 1000 ) #> ℹ Fitting K-gamma model... #> ✔ Fitting K-gamma model... [15ms] #>  #> ℹ Generating 1000 posterior draws... #> ✔ Generating 1000 posterior draws... [25ms] #>  #> Object of class 'pvEBayes' #>  #> K-gamma model with number of gamma mixture components K = 3. #>  #> Running time of the K-gamma model fitting: 0.0198 seconds. #> Optimizer convergence: successful. #> Running time for posterior draws  #> (1000 signal strength posterior draws per AE-drug pair):0.0299 seconds. #>  #> Extract estimated prior parameters, discovered signals #> and signal strength posterior draws using `summary()`.   # fit Efron model with specified hyperparameters # p = 40, c0 = 0.05  fit_efron <- pvEBayes(   contin_table = statin2025_44,   model = "efron",   p = 40,   c0 = 0.05,   n_posterior_draws = 1000 ) #> ℹ Fitting efron model... #> ✔ Fitting efron model... [119ms] #>  #> ℹ Generating 1000 posterior draws... #> ✔ Generating 1000 posterior draws... [30ms] #>  #> Object of class 'pvEBayes' #>  #> efron model is fitted with hyperparameters (p = 40, c0 = 0.05). #>  #> Running time of the efron model fitting: 0.1265 seconds. #> Optimizer convergence: successful. #> Running time for posterior draws  #> (1000 signal strength posterior draws per AE-drug pair):0.037 seconds. #>  #> Extract estimated prior parameters, discovered signals #> and signal strength posterior draws using `summary()`.  # fit GPS model and comapre with 'openEBGM'   fit_gps <- pvEBayes(statin2025_44, model = "GPS") #> ℹ Fitting GPS model... #> ✔ Fitting GPS model... [19ms] #>  #> ℹ Generating 1000 posterior draws... #> ✔ Generating 1000 posterior draws... [28ms] #>  #> Object of class 'pvEBayes' #>  #> GPS (2-gamma) model is fitted #>  #> Running time of the GPS model fitting: 0.0258 seconds. #> Optimizer convergence: successful. #> Running time for posterior draws  #> (1000 signal strength posterior draws per AE-drug pair):0.0342 seconds. #>  #> Extract estimated prior parameters, discovered signals #> and signal strength posterior draws using `summary()`.  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{  ## Optional comparison with openEBGM (only if installed)  ## tol_ecm is the absolute tolerance for ECM stopping rule. ## It is set to ensure comparability to `openEBGM`.  fit_gps <- pvEBayes(statin2025_44, model = "GPS", tol_ecm = 1e-2)  if (requireNamespace("openEBGM", quietly = TRUE)) {   E <- estimate_null_expected_count(statin2025_44)   statin2025_44_stacked <- as.data.frame(as.table(statin2025_44))   statin2025_44_stacked$E <- as.vector(E)   colnames(statin2025_44_stacked) <- c("var1", "var2", "N", "E")   statin2025_44_stacked_squash <- openEBGM::autoSquash(statin2025_44_stacked)    hyper_estimates <- openEBGM::hyperEM(statin2025_44_stacked_squash,     theta_init = c(2, 1, 2, 2, 0.2),     method = "nlminb",     N_star = NULL,     zeroes = TRUE,     param_upper = Inf,     LL_tol = 1e-2,     max_iter = 10000   ) }  theta_hat <- hyper_estimates$estimates qn <- openEBGM::Qn(theta_hat,   N = statin2025_44_stacked$N,   E = statin2025_44_stacked$E )  statin2025_44_stacked$q05 <- openEBGM::quantBisect(5,   theta_hat = theta_hat,   N = statin2025_44_stacked$N,   E = statin2025_44_stacked$E,   qn = qn )  ## obtain the detected signal provided by openEBGM statin2025_44_stacked %>%   subset(q05 > 1.001)  ## detected signal from pvEBayes presented in the same way as openEBGM fit_gps %>%   summary(return = "posterior draws") %>%   apply(c(2, 3), quantile, prob = 0.05) %>%   as.table() %>%   as.data.frame() %>%   subset(Freq > 1.001) } # }

title: Select hyperparameter and obtain the optimal general-gamma or efron model based on AIC and BIC — pvEBayes_tune
text: function performs hyperparameter tuning general-gamma Efron model using AIC BIC. given AE-drug contingency table, function fits series models across grid candidate hyperparameter values computes AIC BIC. models lowest AIC BIC values selected optimal fits.

title: Select hyperparameter and obtain the optimal general-gamma or efron model based on AIC and BIC — pvEBayes_tune
code: pvEBayes_tune(   contin_table,   model = c("general-gamma", "efron"),   alpha_vec = NULL,   p_vec = NULL,   c0_vec = NULL,   use_AIC = TRUE,   n_posterior_draws = 1000,   return_all_fit = FALSE,   return_all_AIC = TRUE,   return_all_BIC = TRUE,   tol_ecm = 1e-04,   rtol_efron = 1e-10,   E = NULL )

title: Select hyperparameter and obtain the optimal general-gamma or efron model based on AIC and BIC — pvEBayes_tune
text: contin_table IxJ contingency table showing pairwise counts adverse events AEs (along rows) J drugs (along columns). model model tuned. Available models "general-gamma" "efron". Default "general-gamma". Note input model case-sensitive. alpha_vec vector hyperparameter alpha values selected. Alpha hyperparameter general-gamma model numeric 0 1. NULL, default set alpha values (0, 0.1, 0.3, 0.5, 0.7, 0.9) used. p_vec vector hyperparameter p values selected. p hyperparameter "efron" model positive integer. NULL, default set p values (40, 60, 80, 100, 120) used. c0_vec vector hyperparameter c0 values selected. c0 hyperparameter "efron" model positive number. NULL, default set c0 values (0.001, 0.01, 0.1, 0.2, 0.5) used. use_AIC logical, indicating whether AIC BIC used. Default TRUE. n_posterior_draws number posterior draws AE-drug combination. return_all_fit logical, indicating whether return fitted model selection. Default FALSE. return_all_AIC logical, indicating whether return AIC values fitted model selection. Default TRUE. return_all_BIC logical, indicating whether return BIC values fitted model selection. Default TRUE. tol_ecm tolerance parameter used ECM stopping rule, defined absolute change joint marginal likelihood two consecutive iterations. used 'GPS', 'K-gamma' 'general-gamma' model fitted. Default 1e-4. rtol_efron tolerance parameter used 'efron' model fitted. Default 1e-10. See 'stats::nlminb' detail. E matrix expected counts null model SRS frequency table. NULL (default), expected counts estimated SRS data using 'estimate_null_expected_count()'.

title: Select hyperparameter and obtain the optimal general-gamma or efron model based on AIC and BIC — pvEBayes_tune
text: function returns S3 object class pvEBayes containing selected estimated model parameters well posterior draws AE-drug combination number posterior draws specified.

text: return = NULL (default), function returns summary table generated summary_table_pvEBayes(), printing fitted pvEBayes object. return specified, function returns requested component: prior parameters list estimated prior parameters. likelihood fitted model log marginal likelihood. detected signal logical matrix indicating AE-drug pairs \(P(\lambda > 1.001 \mid N) > 0.95\). signal detection specified threshold parameters, see 'get_posterior_prob()' posterior draws Posterior draws signal strength AE-drug pair default array format. posterior draws long format Posterior draws signal strength AE-drug pair stacked long format.
